mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls indicates ongoing labor market expansion that supports aggregate income and consumer spending, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month increases in PAYEMS are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of observations over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Once the federal funds rate exceeds 1.0 percent, the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows rises materially, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is above 1.0%, positive 60-day S&P 500 returns occur in more than 50 percent of triggered instances over any 2-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation is elevated and the federal funds rate remains below 1.0 percent, deeply negative real policy rates drive capital reallocation into equities as an inflation hedge, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint occurrences of CPIAUCSL YoY above 5% and FEDFUNDS below 1% are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55 percent of cases, or the interaction term in a linear model is insignificant (|t-stat| < 2).

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, a month-over-month decline in industrial production signals binding supply constraints and impending earnings compression, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 5.0% and INDPRO declines, S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50 percent of instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1,200 thousand units indicates a contraction in residential investment and construction employment that drags down aggregate demand, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST readings below 1200 are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of occurrences over any 2-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP signals a broad-based expansion in economic activity that lifts corporate revenues and profits, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarter-over-quarter increases in GDPC1 are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of observations over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A cumulative increase in the unemployment rate of more than 0.2 percentage points over two months signals a deteriorating labor market that reduces household income and consumption, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: A 0.2 percentage point rise in UNRATE over two months is followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of triggered episodes over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation exceeding 6.0 percent year-over-year forces the Federal Reserve into an aggressive tightening trajectory, rapidly repricing equity risk premiums lower and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL YoY above 6% is followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of triggered months over any 2-year rolling window.
